======================================================================
PAPER-REPORTED RESULTS (Schaper et al. 2025, Research Policy)
======================================================================

--- Table 2, Col. 2: Frontier‑author patents and forward citations ---
PAPER_REPORTED FrontierAuthor coefficient = 0.260 (SE 0.018) ***
PAPER_REPORTED NonFrontierAuthor coefficient = 0.129 (SE 0.009) ***
PAPER_REPORTED Wald test for equality: chi2(2) = 323.41, p < 0.001

--- Table 2, Col. 3: Splitting non‑frontier authors ---
PAPER_REPORTED FrontierAuthor (top‑general, ≤3y) = 0.270 (SE 0.018) ***
PAPER_REPORTED Author, top‑general, >3y = 0.217 (SE 0.014) ***
PAPER_REPORTED Author, top‑field, ≤3y = 0.196 (SE 0.013) ***
PAPER_REPORTED Author, top‑field, >3y = 0.132 (SE 0.015) ***
PAPER_REPORTED Author, no top‑journal = 0.074 (SE 0.010) ***

--- Table 3: Frontier‑author patents and alternative impact ---
PAPER_REPORTED Hit patent [0/1] OLS: FrontierAuthor = 0.035 (0.002) ***
PAPER_REPORTED Hit patent [0/1] OLS: NonFrontierAuthor = 0.018 (0.001) ***
PAPER_REPORTED Internal PatCit10 [0/1]: FrontierAuthor = 0.030 (0.004) ***
PAPER_REPORTED Internal PatCit10 [0/1]: NonFrontierAuthor = 0.018 (0.002) ***
PAPER_REPORTED Ln($-value) OLS: FrontierAuthor = 0.459 (0.025) ***
PAPER_REPORTED Ln($-value) OLS: NonFrontierAuthor = 0.067 (0.016) ***

--- Table 4: Heterogeneous effects (summary from text) ---
PAPER_REPORTED FrontierAuthor premium largest in young scaled‑up firms
PAPER_REPORTED Internal frontier authors: increment ~2/3 larger than other frontier‑author patents
PAPER_REPORTED Young large firms show highest premium for internal frontier authors

--- Table 5: Likelihood of frontier SNPRs (summary from text) ---
PAPER_REPORTED FrontierAuthor patents significantly more likely to reference any science (col.1)
PAPER_REPORTED FrontierAuthor patents contain considerably more SNPRs on average (col.2)
PAPER_REPORTED Conditional on SNPR count, FrontierAuthor patents >2x as likely to cite frontier science (col.3)

--- Supplementary results (mentioned in text) ---
PAPER_REPORTED FrontierAuthor premium holds within firms (Table SM8)
PAPER_REPORTED Strongest in biotechnology fields (Table SM10)
PAPER_REPORTED Frontier SNPR patents receive ~35% more citations than no‑SNPR patents (SM12‑14)
PAPER_REPORTED Priority (first to cite) matters for private value but not for citation impact (SM14)
